title: Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams

seo.title: Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams | Authentic Italian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Italian Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams with our easy recipe! Fresh clams, garlic, white wine, and pasta for a perfect seafood meal.

seo.imageAlt: A plate of authentic Italian Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams with fresh parsley.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a taste of the Italian coast with this exquisite Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams recipe.

description: Experience the true taste of the Italian coast with this classic Spaghetti alle Vongole, featuring tender Manila clams. This beloved seafood pasta dish combines fresh garlic, a splash of white wine, and fragrant parsley to create a light yet incredibly flavorful sauce that perfectly coats al dente spaghetti. A simple masterpiece that brings the essence of Italy to your table.

instructions.title: Prepare the Clams
instructions.text: Ensure clams are purged. Place them in a large bowl of cold salted water for at least 30 minutes, changing water once, to encourage them to expel sand. Discard any clams that are open and do not close when tapped.

instructions.title: Cook the Spaghetti
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Add the spaghetti and cook according to package directions until al dente. Reserve about 1 cup of pasta cooking water before draining.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: While pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a large, deep skillet or pot over medium heat. Add sliced garlic and red pepper flakes (if using) and sauté gently until fragrant, about 2-3 minutes. Do not let the garlic brown.

instructions.title: Add Clams and Wine
instructions.text: Increase heat to medium-high. Add the purged clams to the skillet. Pour in the white wine. Cover the skillet tightly and cook for 5-7 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally, until all the clams have opened. Discard any unopened clams.

instructions.title: Combine Pasta and Sauce
instructions.text: Add the drained al dente spaghetti directly into the skillet with the clams and their liquid. Add half of the chopped fresh parsley. Toss vigorously to combine, allowing the pasta to absorb the flavorful clam sauce. If the sauce seems too dry, add a splash of reserved pasta water.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Continue tossing for 1-2 minutes until the sauce emulsifies and clings beautifully to the spaghetti. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Garnish with the remaining fresh parsley and a drizzle of fresh extra virgin olive oil before serving immediately.

tips: Always purge your clams thoroughly to avoid sandy surprises. A good method is to soak them in cold, salted water for at least 30 minutes, changing the water once.
The key to a great Spaghetti alle Vongole is to cook the spaghetti directly in the clam sauce for the last minute or two. This allows the pasta to absorb all the delicious flavors.
Serve this dish immediately after preparing to enjoy the clams at their freshest and the pasta perfectly coated in sauce. A side of crusty bread is perfect for soaking up any remaining liquid.
article: Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams is a beloved Italian Seafood dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
